title: Data Scientist
description:
Chumley is an early\\-stage tech startup building software that gives tradespeople the flexibility and tools they need to focus on their work, streamlining workflows and keeping more engineers operating at optimum levels. The team is small, ambitious, and focused on shipping practical, high\\-impact tools used in the field every day.
 



  





 Chumley is looking for an AI Engineer to design, build, and ship the AI systems at the core of ChumleyOS. You will work end\\-to\\-end on agents, automations, and intelligent workflows that power real operations, from customer conversations and job dispatching to back\\-office tasks and field engineer support. This is a hands\\-on role where you'll move from prototype to production quickly and see your work used in the field every day.
 



  





**What you'll be doing** 



* Building and deploying LLM\\-powered agents and workflows that automate real operational tasks across customer service, dispatch, and back\\-office functions.
* Designing and implementing RAG pipelines, tool use, function calling, and multi\\-agent orchestration.
* Integrating AI capabilities into the product, with deep connections to Salesforce, internal systems, and third\\-party APIs.
* Prototyping new AI features quickly, evaluating them rigorously, and shipping the ones that work.
* Building evaluation frameworks, prompt versioning, and observability so AI systems stay reliable in production.
* Working closely with the founder and engineering team to identify the highest\\-impact problems AI can solve.
* Staying close to the frontier, bringing in new models, techniques, and tools as the space evolves.



  





**What we're looking for** 



* Hands\\-on experience building with LLMs, whether through OpenAI, Anthropic, open\\-source models, or all of the above.
* Strong software engineering fundamentals, with proficiency in Python and at least one of TypeScript, Node.js, or similar.
* Experience with RAG, vector databases, embeddings, and prompt engineering in production settings.
* Comfort with agent frameworks and tool use patterns (LangChain, LlamaIndex, custom orchestration, MCP, or equivalent).
* Good understanding of evaluation, guardrails, latency, cost, and the operational realities of running AI in production.
* Comfort working in a small, fast\\-moving startup, taking ownership from idea to deployment.



  





**Nice to have** 



* Experience fine\\-tuning models, training embeddings, or working with open\\-source LLMs.
* Familiarity with Salesforce, CRM systems, or operations\\-heavy business software.
* Experience with voice AI, speech\\-to\\-text, or telephony integrations.
* Background in workflow automation, RPA, or no\\-code/low\\-code platforms.
* Previous work on tools for trades, field services, logistics, or other operations\\-heavy environments.
* Open\\-source contributions or side projects in the AI space we can look at.



  





**What you'll get** 



* Real ownership over the AI layer of a product used daily by tradespeople and operations teams.
* The chance to shape AI architecture, tooling, and best practices at an early\\-stage startup.
* Close collaboration with founders and a small, senior team, with direct impact on roadmap and priorities.
* A role that combines frontier AI, real\\-world operations, and meaningful integrations to solve practical problems at scale.
* Budget for compute, tools, and the best models available.



  





 If you enjoy building with AI, like shipping quickly, and want to use frontier tooling to solve real problems for real users, Chumley's AI Engineer role could be a great fit.

title: Senior Test Engineer
description:
**Overview**
**Senior Test Engineer**
**Location:** 
 Hyderabad, India
 **Work Arrangement:** 
 Hybrid – 3 Days a Week in Office
   

  

**About This Role**
 Hyland is looking for an experienced and driven Senior Test Engineer to join our engineering team in Hyderabad, India. In this role, you will be responsible for writing code and scripts, identifying tools for functional and non\\-functional tests, and contributing to automated test frameworks that ensure the delivery of high\\-quality software and products. You will design test plans, procedures, and code/scripts to accurately assess new products and updates in alignment with product requirements and quality standards. You will also collaborate across engineering groups and contribute to the build pipeline. If you are a detail\\-oriented, technically strong engineer who thrives in a fast\\-paced, collaborative environment, we'd love to hear from you.
   

  

**Your Role Responsibilities? Here's What You'll Do.**
* Develop and maintain complex integration, functional, and non\\-functional tests and automated test frameworks across teams to ensure the holistic quality of the software.
* Lead and maintain the implementation of the delivery pipeline, including test automation, frameworks, build environments, test environments, and security.
* Lead the verification of system functionality by executing complex automated and manual tests, analyzing results, and providing recommendations; create and manage performance metrics, defects/enhancements, test cases, and test results within a team.
* Operate as a trusted advisor on issues and trends; provide general consulting services leveraging expertise and best practice knowledge; identify and implement better test tools and automation methodology.
* Complete complex peer code reviews for test automation code and incorporate feedback into automated tests; influence and lead on standards and processes of team and events, including planning, work estimation, peer reviews, and test design.
* Mentor, coach, train, and provide feedback to other team members; research complex escalations for production issues or software issues to ensure the stability of the application.


**Technology Tools**
* Windows/Linux Platforms
* Test Automation Tools
* CI/CD Pipelines
* Source Control Systems
* Scripting Tools
* Database Technology
* Agile Frameworks


**Role Essentials**
* BS or MS in Computer Science or related field (or equivalent experience); 5\\+ years of experience; significant experience with test design, source control systems, and development on Windows/Linux platforms.
* Experience with all phases of the test lifecycle; experience in software application testing tools, methodologies, and process frameworks; experience with test automation and continuous integration tools.
* Significant experience with multiple scripting tools, build environments, and delivery pipelines; significant experience with programming or scripting; experience with database technology and Agile frameworks.
* Strong oral and written communication skills that demonstrate a professional demeanor and the ability to interact with others with discretion and tact; strong collaboration skills applied successfully within team as well as with all levels of employees in other areas.
* Strong critical thinking and problem\\-solving skills; strong leadership, sound judgment, and business acumen; self\\-motivated with the ability to manage projects to completion. Up to 5% travel time required.


**What We'd Like to See (Preferred Skills)**
* Strong at using original thinking to translate goals into the implementation of new ideas and design solutions; demonstrated experience with regression testing.
* Experience documenting standards and practice guidelines; ability to assess the business value and broader impact of software changes when designing automated tests.
* Ability to mentor, coach, and provide constructive feedback to team members; experience providing feedback to leadership on the technical abilities of the team.
* Driven to learn and stay current professionally; able to thrive in a fast\\-paced environment; strong attention to detail.
* Ability to work independently and in a team environment; strong organizational, multi\\-tasking, and time management skills.


**About Hyland**
 Hyland is the pioneer of the Content Innovation Cloud™, delivering ubiquitous enterprise intelligence to organizations with solutions that unlock actionable insights and drive automation. © Hyland. All rights reserved.
   

  

 Trusted by thousands of organizations worldwide, including many of the Fortune 100, Hyland's solutions create the foundation for a connected, agentic enterprise, where teams harness the power of AI to redefine how they operate and engage with those they serve. For additional information on Hyland's platform and services, please visit Hyland.com.
   

  

**\\#HylandLife**
 Since 1991, it has been Hyland's mission to help our employees, customers and partners exceed their potential with our industry‑leading content services platform. Our employees exude a contagious energy and are passionate about what they do – whether it's helping customers succeed, raising up their fellow Hylanders, or engaging in the communities where they live and work.
   

  

 The \\#HylandLife hashtag encompasses our employee‑centric culture. Our employees live our culture day in and day out by bringing their best self to work. Hyland supports them to do just that through career development resources, wellbeing programs and innovation practices. We thrive on diverse viewpoints and new ideas and believe that a positive, inclusive workplace is imperative to sustainable success.
   

  

 As we've grown to a company of nearly 4,000 strong, we have the opportunity to make a significant impact on our communities. We strongly support employee initiatives and align our giving campaigns and programs to organizations that are important to them.
   

  

**Equal Opportunity Statement**
 Hyland is an equal opportunity employer. We value diversity and are committed to providing an inclusive workplace for all employees and applicants. Employment decisions are made without regard to any characteristic protected by applicable laws and regulations. Information collected during the hiring process is used solely to assess qualifications, verify identity, and comply with legal requirements.

title: Senior Software Engineer I
description:

The work you do with our team will directly improve health outcomes by connecting people with the care, pharmacy benefits, data and resources they need to feel their best. Here, you will find a culture guided by inclusion, talented peers, comprehensive benefits and career development opportunities. Come make an impact on the communities we serve as you help us advance health optimization on a global scale. Join us to start
 **Caring. Connecting. Growing together.**
 Optum Technology University (OTU) is hiring a Senior Software Engineer to support large‑scale engineering learning and enablement across Optum. This is not a traditional product or application delivery role. Instead, you will use your hands‑on engineering experience to design, validate, and facilitate practical technical learning \\- including labs, workshops, demos, and reference implementations \\- used by thousands of engineers.
   

  

 In this role, you will help engineers adopt modern development practices, with a solid focus on AI‑assisted coding, cloud‑native development, and secure engineering. You will collaborate with instructional designers, engineering SMEs, and Tiger Teams, and deliver and facilitate hands‑on training sessions, workshops, and live technical labs.
   

  

 This role is ideal for a senior engineer who enjoys teaching, influence, and working at scale.
   

  

**Primary Responsibilities**
* Act as a hands‑on engineering practitioner and technical SME within OTU, supporting software engineering learning initiatives
* Design, review, and maintain sample code, labs, demos, and reference implementations used in OTU programs
* Ensure learning materials reflect current, real‑world engineering practices, not purely theoretical examples
* Contribute to AI‑assisted coding enablement (e.g., Codex, Claude, or similar) by developing:
+ Guardrail‑aligned usage examples
+ Secure and responsible AI development patterns
+ Practical, production‑ready workflows

* Apply enterprise AI and Responsible AI guidance in clear, actionable developer‑facing learning content
* Translate complex technical concepts into clear, practical explanations for engineers at varying experience levels
* Partner with instructional designers to ensure learning content is technically accurate, accessible, and scalable
* Review and contribute to role‑based learning pathways, "what good looks like" examples, and learner readiness guidance
* Design and deliver hands‑on training sessions, workshops, and live labs; facilitate interactive learning experiences such as live coding, walkthroughs, and working sessions
* Pilot, validate, and continuously improve labs and exercises based on learner and facilitator feedback
* Collaborate with Tiger Teams, cloud and security SMEs, and platform/tooling owners to align learning content with enterprise standards and day‑to‑day developer workflows
* Stay current on modern engineering trends and continuously evolve learning content as tools and practices change
* Comply with the terms and conditions of the employment contract, company policies and procedures, and any and all directives (such as, but not limited to, transfer and/or re\\-assignment to different work locations, change in teams and/or work shifts, policies in regards to flexibility of work benefits and/or work environment, alternative work arrangements, and other decisions that may arise due to the changing business environment). The Company may adopt, vary or rescind these policies and directives in its absolute discretion and without any limitation (implied or otherwise) on its ability to do so


**Required Qualifications**
* 7\\+ years of professional software engineering experience
* Solid hands‑on experience with modern development practices (cloud‑native development, APIs, CI/CD pipelines, automated testing)
* Experience working in enterprise‑scale environments with governance, security, and compliance requirements
* Experience supporting engineers during live labs or exercises, including debugging and guidance without taking over execution
* Experience using an LMS (e.g., Cornerstone or similar) to support technical learning or enablement
* Experience curating and maintaining technical content such as documentation, labs, learning modules, or reference materials
* Experience with AI‑assisted development tools or ML‑adjacent workflows, and staying current with evolving capabilities
* Demonstrated experience facilitating technical training, workshops, or hands‑on labs
* Exposure to cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, or GCP) and platform‑based architectures
* Proven ability to write, review, explain, and teach code clearly to a broad engineering audience
* Proven solid communication and collaboration skills; comfortable working across technical and non‑technical partners


**Why This Role**
* Influence how thousands of engineers across Optum learn and adopt modern engineering practices
* Combine hands‑on engineering credibility with teaching and facilitation at scale
* Collaborate with senior engineering, architecture, and security leaders on high‑impact learning initiatives
* Help shape Optum's approach to AI‑enabled software engineering through practical, applied learning
